# Comparison Tables {#comparison-tables}

## Purpose

Side-by-side comparisons of major theories discussed in the book, organized by key dimensions.

---

## Table 1: Consciousness Theories Compared

| Theory | Type | Consciousness Is... | Scope | Testable? | Implies for Life-Consciousness |
|--------|------|---------------------|-------|-----------|-------------------------------|
| GWT | Scientific | Global information broadcast | Brains with global workspace | Yes | Life first |
| IIT | Scientific/philosophical | Integrated information (Φ) | Any system with Φ > 0 | Partially | Co-emergence / panpsychism |
| FEP | Scientific | Byproduct of free energy minimization | All living systems | Partially | Co-emergence |
| AST | Scientific | Model of attention | Any system with attention schema | Yes | Substrate-independent |
| RPT | Scientific | Recurrent neural processing | Nervous systems with feedback | Yes | Life first |
| Orch-OR | Speculative/scientific | Quantum gravitational collapse | Microtubule-containing systems | Partially | Consciousness fundamental |
| Panpsychism | Philosophical | Fundamental property of matter | All matter | No (currently) | Consciousness first |
| Idealism | Philosophical | The only reality | Everything | No (currently) | Consciousness first |
| Emergentism | Philosophical | Emergent property of complexity | Complex brains | Indirectly | Life first |

## Table 2: Origin of Life Models and Consciousness Implications

| Model | Key Mechanism | Implies Agency? | Consciousness Relevance |
|-------|---------------|----------------|------------------------|
| RNA World | Self-replicating RNA | Minimal | Low — purely chemical |
| Metabolism-First | Self-sustaining chemical cycles | Moderate | Moderate — self-organization |
| Protocells | Lipid compartmentalization | Moderate | Moderate — self/non-self boundary |
| Autocatalytic Sets | Collective catalysis | Moderate | Moderate — systemic self-maintenance |
| Dissipative Structures | Energy-driven organization | Moderate | Moderate — thermodynamic agency |
| Assembly Theory | Molecular complexity | Low | Low — detection, not explanation |
| Autopoiesis | Self-production | High | High — implies minimal cognition |

## Table 3: Where Each Theory Places the Transition

| Theory | Transition Point | Gradual or Sudden? | Earliest Conscious Entity |
|--------|------------------|--------------------|--------------------------|
| GWT | Global workspace architecture | Sudden | Complex-brained animals |
| IIT | Φ > 0 | Gradual | Possibly any integrated system |
| FEP | Any free-energy-minimizing system | Gradual | All living systems |
| RPT | Recurrent processing | Sudden | Animals with recurrent circuits |
| Orch-OR | Quantum coherence in microtubules | Sudden | Organisms with microtubules |
| Panpsychism | Always present | N/A | All matter |
| Emergentism | Sufficient complexity | Sudden | Complex nervous systems |

## Table 4: Philosophical Frameworks Compared

| Framework | Mind-Matter Relation | Life-Consciousness Relation | Key Proponent |
|-----------|---------------------|----------------------------|---------------|
| Substance Dualism | Separate substances | Separate questions | Descartes |
| Physicalism | Mind = physical | Life first | Smart, Armstrong |
| Functionalism | Mind = function | Substrate-independent | Putnam, Fodor |
| Panpsychism | Mind in all matter | Consciousness first/co-emergence | Strawson, Goff |
| Idealism | Only mind exists | Consciousness first | Berkeley, Kastrup |
| Neutral Monism | Both from a third thing | Co-emergence | Russell, James |
| Dual-Aspect Monism | Two aspects of one | Co-emergence | Spinoza, Pauli-Jung |
| Process Philosophy | Experience is fundamental | Co-emergence | Whitehead |

[Additional tables can be added as the book develops]
